body:
For an ImmersedBoundaryGrid, the `NetCDFWriter` exports `peripheral_nodes_{ccc,fcc,cfc,ccf}` as the land/immersed mask, which (on a Bounded direction) masks the wall face as `peripheral`. At an open boundary (i.e. with a `NormalFlow` BC) that face is of interest so we should have a mask written to NetCDF that allows inactive cells (like immersed boundaries) to be masked, but that don't mask these wall cells. The `inactive_nodes*` masks does that, so this PR adds it to `NetCDFWriter`'s `gather_immersed_boundary()` function.

```julia
using Oceananigans
using Oceananigans.Grids: peripheral_node, inactive_node

grid = RectilinearGrid(size=(4, 4, 4), x=(0, 1), y=(0, 1), z=(-1, 0), topology=(Bounded, Bounded, Bounded))

peripheral_node(1, 2, 2, grid, Face(), Center(), Center())   # true  ← wall flagged as periphery
inactive_node(1, 2, 2, grid, Face(), Center(), Center())    # false ← interior cell is active
```
title: Add `inactive_nodes` to `NetCDFWriter`'s `gather_immersed_boundary`
